reason: Provenance gap on GT-binomial-series. The node uses the value sum_k |C(-1/2,k)| t^k = (1-t)^(-1/2) (it writes '=(1-||U||)^(-1/2)' for the absolute sum and again for the tail). This exact analytic identity has NO source in refs/: the external GT-binomial-series itself states 'PROVENANCE GAP: no standalone analysis source in refs/'. The cited fallback, Kitaev refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:503-510 (eqs Taylor_simple/Taylor_simple_bound), provides only the abstract Taylor series f(X)=a_0 I+sum a_n (X-x_0 I)^n and the bound ||f(X)-f(x_0 I)|| <= sum_{n>=1} |a_n| ||X-x_0 I||^n. It does NOT evaluate the coefficient sum sum |a_n| t^n nor identify it as (1-t)^(-1/2). Under the project rule 'there are no standard facts', the closed-form value of the absolute binomial sum is an uncited analysis fact and must be provenanced.
